concept.title: Creating a Purchase Order in SAP ECC 6.0
concept.shortdesc: This topic describes how to create a standard purchase order using transaction ME21N in SAP ECC 6.0 Materials Management.

section.title: Overview
section.p: Purchase orders in SAP ECC are created using the classic SAP GUI transaction ME21N. The system automatically calculates taxes based on the tax code maintained in the vendor master. This functionality has been available since SAP R/3 4.6C.
Note: SAP Business Suite users should ensure they are running ECC 6.0 Enhancement Package 3 or higher for the features described in this topic.

ul.li: Access to SAP GUI 7.10 or later
Vendor master data maintained in transaction XK01
Material master data with purchasing view activated
Purchasing organization and purchasing group configured

step.cmd: Log on to SAP GUI and enter transaction code ME21N in the command field.
step.info: The Create Purchase Order screen opens.

step.cmd: In the Vendor field, enter the vendor number or use F4 to search.
Select the Purchasing Org. from the dropdown and enter the Purch. Group.
In the item overview area, enter the Material number, Quantity, and Plant.
step.info: The system will propose the price from the last purchase order or info record. Price conditions are determined using the pricing procedure XXXXXX.

step.cmd: Click the Save button or press Ctrl+S.
step.result: A purchase order number is generated. Note this number for follow-up activities.

section.title: Account Assignment
section.p: For cost center purchasing, select account assignment category K and enter the cost center in the Account Assignment tab. The G/L account is determined automatically from the valuation class in the material master.
Project-related purchasing uses account assignment category P with a WBS element from PS (Project System).

section.title: Follow-Up Activities
section.p: After saving the purchase order, you can:

ul.li: Print the purchase order using transaction ME9F
Send by fax using the output condition type NEU
Create a goods receipt using transaction MIGO
Monitor the purchase order in transaction ME2M

section.title: Troubleshooting
section.p: If you receive the error message "No purchasing info record exists", you must first create an info record using transaction ME11 before creating the purchase order.
Authorization errors typically indicate missing authorization object M_BEST_BSA. Contact your Basis administrator to resolve authorization issues.
